key_insight: High-impact hepatocellular carcinoma research shifted in 2024 from broad immunotherapy toward more specific combination regimens and precision-diagnostics themes.
clinical_relevance: This bibliometric trend may help researchers and developers spot where HCC treatment innovation is concentrating, especially around dual checkpoint blockade and imaging-driven approaches.
method: Compared the 50 most cited HCC papers from 2023 and 2024 using OpenAlex citation data.
Used large language models to extract, normalize, and classify concepts from paper text into canonical topics and parent themes.
Compared year-over-year topic frequencies to identify changing research priorities.
evidence_signal: The analysis covered 100 influential papers selected by eighteen-month post-publication citation counts, providing a focused but citation-based snapshot of high-impact HCC literature.
watch_item: Because the sample is limited to highly cited papers and relies on AI topic classification, emerging or niche themes may be undercounted or misgrouped.
paper_title: Mapping Topic Change in Influential Hepatocellular Carcinoma Research: A Two-Cohort Bibliometric Analysis
